entries.definition:
occur 31 times in the Qurʾan: غَلَبَ ghalaba 11 times; غُلِبَ ghuliba four times; غَلَب ghalab once; غَالِب ghālib three times; غَالِبُون ghālibūn 10 times; مَغْلُوب maghlūb once and غُلْب ghulb once.
غَلَبَ ghalaba i [v. trans.] 1 to defeat (8:66) فَإِنْ يَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ مِائَةٌ صَابِرَةٌ يَغْلِبُوا مِائَتَيْنِ should there be a steadfast hundred of you, they will defeat two hundred 2 [with prep. عَلَى] to prevail over, to overpower, to overwhelm (23:106) قَالُوا رَبَّنَا غَلَبَتْ عَلَيْنَا شِقْوَتُنَا they will say, ‘Lord, our misfortune has overwhelmed us!’

غُلِبَ ghuliba [pass. v.] to be defeated, to be overpowered, to be overcome (3:12) سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلَى جَهَنَّمَ you will be overcome and gathered into Hell.

غَلَب ghalab [v. n.] defeating, prevailing over (30:3) وَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ but, after having been defeated, they will prevail.

غَالِب ghālib [act. part.; pl. غَالِبُون ghālibūn] one who is victorious, one who overcomes, one who defeats (3:160) إِنْ يَنْصُرْكُمُ اللَّهُ فَلَا غَالِبَ لَكُمْ if God helps you, no one can overcome you.

مَغْلُوب maghlūb [pass. part.] one who is defeated, overcome, subdued, vanquished (54:10) فَدَعَا رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانْتَصِرْ and so he called upon his Lord, ‘I am vanquished, so help me.’

غُلْب ghulb [pl. of fem. quasi-act. part. غَلْبَاء ghalbāʾ] luscious or abounding in trees thick with branches and leaves (80:30) وَحَدَائِقَ غُلْبًا and luscious gardens.
entries.definition_english:
occur 31 times in the Qurʾan: غَلَبَ ghalaba 11 times; غُلِبَ ghuliba four times; غَلَب ghalab once; غَالِب ghālib three times; غَالِبُون ghālibūn 10 times; مَغْلُوب maghlūb once and غُلْب ghulb once.

entries.definition:
gh–l–ẓ to be thick, to be gross, to be strong; to be severe, to be harsh, to be firm. Of this root, five forms occur 13 times in the Qurʾan: أُغْلُظ ughluẓ twice; اِسْتَغْلَظَ ʾistaghlaẓa once; غَلِيظ ghalīẓ eight times; غِلَاظ ghilāẓ once and غِلْظَة ghilẓatun once.
أُغْلُظ ughluẓ [imper. of v. غَلُظَ ghaluẓa, intrans.] be harsh, be strong, be firm (9:73) يَاأَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جَاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ وَاغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ Prophet, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be severe with them.

اِسْتَغْلَظَ ʾistaghlaẓa [v. X, intrans.] to become thick, to thicken, to gain thickness (48:29) كَزَرْعٍ أَخْرَجَ شَطْأَهُ فَآزَرَهُ فَاسْتَغْلَظَ فَاسْتَوَى عَلَى سُوقِهِ as seeds that put forth their shoots, and [the earth] strengthens

673

gh–l–ẓ to be thick, to be gross, to be strong; to be severe, to be harsh, to be firm. Of this root, five forms occur 13 times in the Qurʾan: أُغْلُظ ughluẓ twice; اِسْتَغْلَظَ ʾistaghlaẓa once; غَلِيظ ghalīẓ eight times; غِلَاظ ghilāẓ once and غِلْظَة ghilẓatun once.

673
